description_long: This is a sleek, futuristic fighter-style airplane with a sharply pointed nose and a compact, angular fuselage. The wings are swept back and integrated into the body, featuring red accent markings along their edges and surfaces. Three large, circular jet exhausts dominate the rear, suggesting powerful propulsion, while the body is finished in a weathered metallic gray with visible panel lines and mechanical details. The cockpit is forward-mounted and enclosed with a dark canopy, providing a streamlined profile. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from the rear. The overall design is reminiscent of science fiction or advanced military aircraft, emphasizing speed and agility.
description: A futuristic, angular fighter airplane with swept-back wings, three large rear jet engines, and a forward-enclosed cockpit. The body is metallic gray with red accents and a conventional tail.
description_view_0: Rear view showing three prominent circular jet exhausts and the swept-back wings with red markings.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the angular fuselage, forward cockpit, and detailed engine housing.
description_view_2: Front view emphasizing the pointed nose, forward canopy, and symmetrical wing design.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to the left, showing the engine arrangement and tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back configuration with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles; wings are integrated into the fuselage for a streamlined appearance.
attribute_engine_type: Triple rear-mounted jet engines, circular exhausts visible at the back of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers extending from the rear fuselage.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear under the forward fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single-seat canopy integrated into the nose section of the fuselage.
